title: Fix CHIRP CSV Import — Persist All 4000+ Repeaters to Canister

requirements.summary: Update CHIRP CSV parser to correctly map all CHIRP CSV columns and normalize CTCSS/PL tone fields for reliable bulk imports
acceptanceCriteria: A CHIRP CSV with 4,000+ rows produces 4,000+ parsed Repeater objects with no silent parse failures.
CTCSS/PL tone values are correctly normalized for all rows.
City and state fields are correctly extracted for all 50 US states.
Rows that cannot be parsed (missing required fields) are counted and reported as skipped without crashing the import.

file_operations.description: Enhance the parseChirpCsv function to handle all CHIRP CSV column mappings including Location, Name, Frequency, Duplex, Offset, Tone, rToneFreq, cToneFreq, DtcsCode, Mode, and Comment. Improve CTCSS/PL tone normalization to consistently convert string values like '100.0' into the integer format expected by the backend Repeater type. Strengthen city/state extraction logic to reliably handle all 50 US state names and abbreviations across multiple possible column formats. Add error tracking to count and report rows that fail parsing due to missing required fields, ensuring the parser never crashes on malformed rows.

requirements.summary: Update Admin CSV import page to batch-upload all parsed repeaters with progress feedback and detailed import statistics
acceptanceCriteria: Uploading a CHIRP CSV with 4,000+ repeaters triggers batched canister writes.
A progress indicator (e.g., 'Saving batch 3 of 20...') is shown during the upload.
On completion, a summary shows total saved, total skipped, and any errors.
All 4,000+ repeaters appear in the directory page after a successful import.

file_operations.description: Refactor the CSV import flow to call the backend bulkAddRepeaters function in batches (e.g., 200 records per batch). Add a progress indicator component that displays the current batch number and total batches during upload (e.g., 'Saving batch 3 of 20...'). After all batches complete, display a comprehensive summary showing total repeaters successfully saved, number skipped due to parse errors, and any backend errors encountered. Ensure the UI remains responsive during large imports and provides clear feedback at each stage. After successful import, invalidate the repeater directory query cache to trigger a refresh.

Add a useBulkAddRepeaters mutation hook that wraps the backend bulkAddRepeaters function. The hook should accept an array of Repeater objects and return mutation state (loading, error, success). Include proper error handling to capture and surface backend authorization or storage errors. Ensure the mutation invalidates all repeater-related queries (approved repeaters, directory cache) on success so the UI reflects the newly imported data.

requirements.summary: Fix repeater directory page to load and display all stored repeaters without pagination or query limits
acceptanceCriteria: The directory page loads all stored repeaters (not a hard-coded or small limit).
Infinite scroll or pagination loads subsequent pages until all records are accessible.
Search and filter (by state, mode, call sign) work across the full dataset.
The result count displayed matches the actual total stored in the canister.

file_operations.description: Review and fix the infinite scroll or pagination logic to ensure all stored repeaters are loaded and displayed, not just the first 17 or a hard-coded subset. Verify that the useInfiniteQuery hook (if used) correctly fetches subsequent pages until all records are retrieved. Ensure the result count displayed matches the actual total from the backend. Confirm that client-side filtering (by search term, state, mode) operates on the full loaded dataset. If the directory uses the getApprovedRepeaters backend method, ensure it returns all approved repeaters without arbitrary limits.

Review the useApprovedRepeaters or directory query hook to ensure it fetches the complete set of repeaters from the backend without imposing client-side or query-side limits. If using infinite scroll, verify that the page fetching logic continues until all records are loaded. If the backend returns a fixed array, ensure the query does not truncate or paginate unnecessarily. Add a total count return value or query metadata to expose the full dataset size for UI display.

requirements.summary: Require Internet Identity authentication to access CSV import functionality with admin-only guard
acceptanceCriteria: Visiting the import/admin page without being logged in shows a login prompt.
After logging in with Internet Identity, the CSV upload UI is accessible.
Non-admin authenticated users cannot trigger bulk imports.
Admin users can upload and import CSV files successfully.

file_operations.description: Add authentication and authorization guards to the AdminPage component. Use the authorization component to verify the user is logged in via Internet Identity and has admin role before displaying the CSV import UI. If the user is not logged in, render a login prompt or redirect to the login flow. If the user is authenticated but not an admin, display an AccessDeniedScreen or equivalent message. Ensure the CSV upload and bulk import actions are only accessible to authenticated admin users. Verify the component's usage instructions for the authorization component before implementing.

Ensure the AdminDashboardPage component uses the same authorization guard as AdminPage to enforce admin-only access. If the user is not logged in, render a login prompt. If the user is authenticated but not an admin, display an AccessDeniedScreen. This ensures consistent authorization enforcement across all admin routes. Verify the component's usage instructions for the authorization component before implementing.

Update the navigation links in the Header component to conditionally show admin-related links only when the current user is authenticated and has admin role. Use the authorization component's hooks to check the user role and hide or disable admin links for non-admin users. This prevents unauthorized users from navigating to admin pages via the UI. Verify the component's usage instructions for the authorization component before implementing.
